intro: Migrating from JavaScript to Scala transitions a codebase from a dynamically typed, single-threaded event loop environment to a statically typed, multi-paradigm language running on the JVM. Developers make this shift to leverage Scala's powerful type system, functional programming primitives, and robust concurrency models for high-throughput backend services. This move eliminates runtime type errors common in JavaScript while introducing advanced features like pattern matching and implicits.

diffs.from: Dynamically typed with implicit type coercion and runtime resolution.
diffs.to: Statically typed with advanced type inference, structural typing, and generalized type constraints.

diffs.from: C-style syntax heavily reliant on functions, object literals, and prototype chains.
diffs.to: Expression-oriented syntax blending object-oriented classes with functional constructs like traits and case classes.

diffs.from: Managed by host environment engine (V8, JavaScriptCore) using a generational garbage collector.
diffs.to: Managed by the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) using highly configurable collectors like G1GC or ZGC.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop utilizing Promises, async/await keyword pairs, and callbacks.
diffs.to: Multi-threaded concurrency managed via Futures, ExecutionContexts, and functional effect systems like Cats Effect or ZIO.

diffs.from: Minimal core focusing on prototype primitives, arrays, and basic math; heavily relies on npm.
diffs.to: Extensive core standard library with powerful collections, functional combinators, and native multi-threading primitives.

examples.title: Data Transformation
examples.description: Filtering and mapping a list of user objects based on attributes.
examples.fromCode:
const users = [{ name: 'Alice', active: true }, { name: 'Bob', active: false }];
const activeNames = users.filter(u => u.active).map(u => u.name);
examples.toCode:
case class User(name: String, active: Boolean)
val users = List(User("Alice", true), User("Bob", false))
val activeNames: List[String] = users.filter(_.active).map(_.name)

examples.title: Asynchronous Computation Chain
examples.description: Fetching a resource and transforming the result asynchronously.

tips.title: Embrace Expression-Oriented Design
tips.body: In Scala, almost every construct (including if-else blocks and try-catch) returns a value. Avoid defining mutable variable placeholders and reassigning them; instead, assign the outcome of the conditional or loop directly to an immutable val.

tips.title: Replace Prototypes with Traits
tips.body: JavaScript's prototype-based inheritance does not exist in Scala. Use traits to define shared interfaces and modular behavior that can be mixed into classes and objects at compile time.

tips.title: Isolate Side Effects via Options
tips.body: Instead of returning null or undefined for missing data, wrap results in Scala's Option (Some/None) monad. This forces compile-time handling of empty states and eliminates NullPointerExceptions.

tips.title: Understand ExecutionContexts
tips.body: Unlike JavaScript's automatic global event loop, Scala requires an explicit ExecutionContext to execute Futures. Ensure you scope your implicits correctly to avoid leaking thread pools across application boundaries.
